defects.symptom: `equip install <plugin>@<marketplace>` fails 'not found in known marketplaces' for a marketplace Claude Code already knows — declared in the very .claude/settings.json install writes to (extraKnownMarketplaces), in ~/.claude/plugins/known_marketplaces.json, and cloned under ~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/. Took 3+ CLI invocations and ended with Claude hand-faking the equipment.json record (evidence items 1, 22; also why uninstall/repair can never reach a natively-added marketplace).
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. `_collect_catalogs` (cli/equip/discover.py:76-149) builds the entire candidate universe from exactly three sources: hardcoded SEED_MARKETPLACES (marketplace.py:43-50), live `gh search` on the query, and explicit `--repo`. A repo-wide grep shows nothing in the package ever READS extraKnownMarketplaces (claude_plugins.py only writes it), ~/.claude/plugins/known_marketplaces.json, installed_plugins.json, or the on-disk marketplace clones. run_install then resolves the target only against that universe (discover.py:330-334) and errors at :351.
defects.proposed_fix: Add a 'locally declared marketplaces' catalog source admitted right after seeds (before gh-search results, so it wins name collisions). (1) New CLI-boundary reader — e.g. `list_marketplaces(settings_path)` in context/claude_plugins.py reusing load_settings — collecting name→repo from project .claude/settings.json, .claude/settings.local.json, ~/.claude/settings.json (extraKnownMarketplaces), and ~/.claude/plugins/known_marketplaces.json (shape {name:{source:{source:'github',repo},installLocation,...}}). (2) Prefer parsing the on-disk clone's .claude-plugin/marketplace.json at installLocation / ~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/<name>/ (no network), falling back to fetch_catalog(repo). (3) In run_install, when the requested <marketplace> name exactly matches a declared marketplace, resolve it directly by name instead of through the query-scoring path. Use a frozen dataclass (e.g. DeclaredMarketplace) per conventions; keep them trusted=False. Design decision: whether a committed-settings marketplace counts as trusted for the approval gate — recommend no, pair with the prior-approval defect below.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/domains/equip/test_equip_discover_cli.py (existing fake-runner idiom — monkeypatch dummyindex.cli.equip.discover._RUNNER): (a) project settings.json declares extraKnownMarketplaces for a repo the fake gh search never returns; assert install <plugin>@<that-marketplace> succeeds without --repo; (b) same via fake ~/.claude/plugins/known_marketplaces.json with monkeypatched Path.home; (c) fake runner records argv — assert the local clone's marketplace.json was used with zero `gh api` calls; (d) discover surfaces the declared marketplace's plugins for a matching query.

defects.symptom: Plugin wired by equip never actually loads in Claude Code: install claims success, plugin missing from the running app; user cycles /plugin + /reload-plugins; '/plugin Errors' shows failed marketplace download (evidence items 3, 35, 41; compounds item 4).
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. cli/equip/discover.py:370 passes the PLUGIN's listing semver as the MARKETPLACE repo's git ref: `add_marketplace(settings, name=chosen.marketplace, repo=chosen.repo, ref=chosen.plugin.version)` — writing e.g. {source:{source:'github',repo:'pbakaus/impeccable',ref:'3.5.0'}} (the exact entry in evidence item 41). Verified live: pbakaus/impeccable's tags are 'skill-v3.5.0' etc. — no '3.5.0' ref exists, so Claude Code's clone of the marketplace fails and the plugin never registers in installed_plugins.json. Intermittent because most catalog entries have version=None (no ref written). The same value also lands in manifest origin_ref, documented as 'pinned commit sha' (models.py:106) — a second wrong use.
defects.proposed_fix: Drop `ref=chosen.plugin.version` from the add_marketplace call at cli/equip/discover.py:370 (matching Claude Code's own known_marketplaces.json entries, which carry no ref). Record the plugin's catalog semver in the manifest item's `version` field instead (see manifest defect). If supply-chain pinning is wanted later, resolve a real marketplace HEAD sha (`gh api repos/<repo>/commits/HEAD`) into origin_ref as a separate, optional step — never the plugin semver.
defects.tests_to_add: test_equip_discover_cli.py: install a plugin whose fake catalog entry has version='3.5.0'; assert the written settings extraKnownMarketplaces entry has NO 'ref' key, and the manifest item records version='3.5.0' with origin_ref=None. Regression test in tests/context/test_claude_plugins.py that add_marketplace without ref omits the key.

defects.symptom: Manifest entry for an installed plugin is near-useless for plan/build '— via' routing: kind 'agent' (it is a plugin), capabilities [] or bogus, version null (equip status renders '(v-)'), origin_hash/subagent_type null; no flag to set capabilities at install time, so Claude hand-edited equipment.json (evidence items 6, 18, 24, 33).
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. _record_native (cli/equip/discover.py:390-409) hardcodes kind=EquipmentKind.AGENT at :397 — EquipmentKind (enums.py:7-13) has no PLUGIN member. capabilities=chosen.capabilities comes straight from the substring keyword inference (often () or wrong, see capability defect); `version` is never set (only origin_ref=chosen.plugin.version), so run_status prints '(v-)' via `version or "-"` (verbs.py:66-67). run_install's flag set (discover.py:304-315) has no --capabilities override. subagent_type is never resolvable because install never inspects the plugin's actual agents/commands.
defects.proposed_fix: (1) Add EquipmentKind.PLUGIN = 'plugin' (enums.py) and use it in _record_native; from_dict already round-trips via EquipmentKind(data['kind']) so only the new member is needed — verify status/uninstall paths key off source==MARKETPLACE (they do: status.py:155-158, :307-311), so classification is unaffected. (2) Set version=chosen.plugin.version on the item so status shows the real semver. (3) Add `--capabilities a,b` flag to run_install, validated against the Capability enum (typed error on unknown value), overriding inference when given. (4) Optional follow-up (design decision, coordinate with C7-build-routing): a `--subagent-type` flag so a plugin's dispatchable agent can be recorded without JSON surgery. Note schema_version stays 3 — all new/changed fields are tolerated by the loader.
defects.tests_to_add: test_equip_manifest_v3.py: EquipmentItem round-trip with kind='plugin'. test_equip_discover_cli.py: install writes kind 'plugin', version from catalog entry, and honors --capabilities database,frontend (rejecting --capabilities bogus with rc 2); run_status output shows '(v3.5.0)' not '(v-)'.

defects.symptom: Discover's 'covers' capability tags are nonsense and inconsistent across copies of the same plugin (figma covers: database; superpowers covers: database/security/frontend on one marketplace, '-' on another; impeccable tagged 'security'), and free-text queries return walls of irrelevant plugins ('design to code bridge' ranks migration-to-aws, 42crunch) (evidence items 21, 27, 31).
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED and reproduced against the real anthropics/claude-plugins-official catalog: capabilities_for (plugins/discover.py:32-41) does bare substring matching over name+description+keywords+category — 'orm' (DATABASE row, constants.py:74) matches inside 'platform'/'information'/'brainstorming' (hence figma→database, superpowers→database), 'audit' sits in the SECURITY row (constants.py:75) so design-audit plugins tag security, 'ui' matches inside 'guides'/'builds' (→frontend). Inconsistency across marketplaces follows directly: caps derive from each marketplace's listing prose, not the plugin. _query_hits (:44-46) counts the same substring hits per query token with no stopword filter — 'to' and 'code' match nearly every entry (reproduced: top hits for 'design to code bridge' include 42crunch-api-security-testing).
defects.proposed_fix: In capabilities_for and _query_hits, tokenize the haystack on non-alphanumerics and match whole words, allowing explicit prefix tokens where intended (e.g. 'optimi', 'profil' match as prefixes — represent as ('optimi*',) or a small (token, prefix?) tuple). Move 'audit' out of the SECURITY row (it already lives in REVIEW). Drop query tokens shorter than 3 chars and a small stopword set ('to','a','an','the','and','or','for','of','in','with','on'). Weight a query hit in the plugin NAME above a description hit (e.g. 2x) so the obviously-named plugin outranks prose matches. Keep the function pure and the table in constants.py; this is a behavior change to ranking — update test_equip_discover.py expectations deliberately.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/domains/equip/test_equip_discover.py: (a) entry with description 'design platform integration ... information' yields NO database capability; (b) 'design audit and review' yields review but not security; (c) prefix tokens still work ('optimization'→performance); (d) query 'design to code bridge' does not score an entry whose description merely contains 'to' and 'code'; (e) name-match outranks description-match for the same query.

defects.symptom: `discover --repo owner/name` never visibly surfaces the requested repo: `--repo obra/superpowers` returned a generic marketplace dump with no obra/superpowers row, and the footer still advised 'add --repo' (evidence items 2, 30).
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED, four compounding causes, two REPRODUCED live: (1) _print_plan (cli/equip/discover.py:282-285) prints only plugin@marketplace, never Candidate.repo and never a 'pinned' marker — obra/superpowers' marketplace.json declares name 'superpowers-dev' (verified via gh api), so when the pin works the row reads 'superpowers@superpowers-dev', indistinguishable from a random fork (reproduced in a temp dir). (2) With no query, needed caps come from the stack (:244) and pinned-repo entries scoring 0 are silently dropped by match_candidates (domain discover.py:68) — reproduced: `discover --repo obra/superpowers` alone prints 'no matching plugins found. Tip: ... add --repo' (:272-274). (3) extra_repos are admitted LAST (:133-148), after gh-search results, so _admit's name dedupe (:102-107) drops the explicit repo whenever a search-result fork claims the same marketplace name first. (4) The footer (:288) unconditionally suggests --repo even when it was passed.
defects.proposed_fix: (1) Always force-include pinned-repo candidates: pass the pinned repo set into the CLI layer's plan assembly and append any candidate whose c.repo is pinned even at score 0 (or give match_candidates a `pinned_repos: frozenset[str]` param that bypasses the score<=0 drop for them — keep it pure). (2) Reorder _collect_catalogs: admit extra_repos immediately after seeds, before gh-search results, so the explicit repo wins name collisions. (3) Print the source repo on each row (`{plugin}@{marketplace}  [{repo}]`) and a `pinned` marker for --repo rows; print one confirmation line: 'resolved --repo owner/name as marketplace <name> (N plugins)'. (4) Thread a `repo_given: bool` into _print_plan to suppress both --repo tips when the flag was used. Same _print_plan changes apply to the JSON output (add 'repo' key).
defects.tests_to_add: test_equip_discover_cli.py: (a) discover --repo with NO query and an empty .context lists the pinned repo's plugins (no 'no matching plugins found'); (b) fake gh search returns a fork claiming the same marketplace name — assert the --repo catalog still wins and its rows show the pinned repo; (c) output contains 'resolved --repo' and does NOT contain the 'add --repo' tip when --repo was passed; (d) row output includes the owner/repo string.

defects.symptom: Trust gate blocks installing a plugin the team already approved: re-registering canvas-to-code — already in enabledPlugins in the committed .claude/settings.json — still errored 'requires approval (untrusted source). Re-run with --yes' (evidence item 23).
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. requires_approval = not candidate.trusted (install_plan.py:42) classifies purely by source trust tier; run_install (cli/equip/discover.py:354-362) gates on it without ever reading the existing enabledPlugins of the settings file it is about to write — a plugin the team already enabled in the committed settings.json is re-gated as if brand new.
defects.proposed_fix: In run_install (CLI boundary, keeping the domain pure): before the approval gate, load the COMMITTED project .claude/settings.json via load_settings and if enabledPlugins['<plugin>@<marketplace>'] is already true, skip the --yes requirement and print a note ('already team-approved in .claude/settings.json — re-registering'). Design decision (security-relevant): only the committed settings.json counts as prior approval — settings.local.json and user scope do not, since they are not team-reviewed; document this in the SKILL.md trust section.
defects.tests_to_add: test_equip_discover_cli.py: pre-seed tmp project settings.json with enabledPlugins for the untrusted target; assert install succeeds WITHOUT --yes and prints the re-registration note; counter-test: same key only in settings.local.json still requires --yes.

defects.symptom: Install declares success when the plugin is merely DECLARED, not loaded: no check against ~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json, no 'restart/reload required' guidance, no clone-transport preflight — so an SSH-only fetch failure and a stale clone surfaced only later in the /plugin UI, and the user manually cycled /plugin + /reload-plugins (evidence items 3, 4 (mitigation only), 35).
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. run_install ends at the success print (cli/equip/discover.py:386) immediately after writing settings keys. A repo-wide grep finds no reference to installed_plugins.json, no reload/restart messaging anywhere in equip code or SKILL.md, and no transport probe — settings-key declaration is conflated with the plugin being loaded, which only happens at session start/reload and only if Claude Code's own fetch (SSH-first on this machine) succeeds.
defects.proposed_fix: (1) Always append an activation line after the success print: 'Claude Code loads plugins at session start — restart the session or run /reload-plugins, then verify with /plugin.' (2) Add a cheap preflight before wiring: `git ls-remote https://github.com/<owner>/<repo> HEAD` via the existing _RUNNER seam; on failure warn (do not abort) that the native fetch may fail and why. (3) Best-effort post-check: if ~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json exists and lacks the key, report status 'declared (not yet loaded)' instead of implying it is live; this read also feeds the duplicate-scope defect. Design decision: warn-only vs --strict abort for the preflight — recommend warn-only to keep install usable offline.
defects.tests_to_add: test_equip_discover_cli.py: (a) successful install output contains the reload/restart guidance line; (b) fake runner fails the ls-remote probe — output contains a transport warning and install still exits 0; (c) with a fake installed_plugins.json lacking the key, output says 'declared (not yet loaded)'.

defects.symptom: Discover results differ between consecutive runs of the identical query (~1 min apart): marketplaces appear/disappear (bg-superpowers gone; superpowers-copilot/-dev appear), making selection unreproducible and masking whether --repo changed anything (evidence item 32).
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. search_github (sources.py:99-122) runs a live `gh search code` with no --limit and no sort — GitHub code-search ranking/result-set is inherently unstable and secondary-rate-limited, and a non-zero exit silently falls back to `gh search repos` (a different result universe). Each discovered repo is then fetched live; _fetch_one (cli/equip/discover.py:62-74) returns None on ANY EquipError/missing catalog, silently shrinking the candidate set. The domain sort (plugins/discover.py:81) is deterministic only for a given candidate set — the set itself churns. Verified live: `gh search code .claude-plugin/marketplace.json superpowers` returned a 15-repo slice not even containing obra/superpowers.
defects.proposed_fix: (1) Add `--limit 30` to the code search and sort the deduped repo list lexicographically before fetching, so the same gh response always yields the same fetch order and (after dedupe-by-name) the same admissions. (2) Count fetch misses in _collect_catalogs and print one summary warning ('N discovered marketplaces unreachable — results may be partial') so a rate-limited/flaky run is visibly partial rather than silently different. (3) Design decision (bigger lever, optional): cache fetched catalogs under ~/.cache/dummyindex/ with a short TTL so back-to-back runs are identical; if added, keep all I/O behind the Runner/CLI boundary per conventions.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/domains/equip/test_equip_sources.py: search_github passes --limit and returns a sorted, deduped tuple for a fixed fake response. test_equip_discover_cli.py: fake runner fails 2 of 4 catalog fetches — output contains the single 'unreachable' summary warning and still lists the reachable candidates.

defects.symptom: Scope chaos with zero cross-scope awareness: one plugin lands in committed settings.json, the next in gitignored settings.local.json; the committed equipment.json points at the gitignored file (teammates get a manifest claiming a plugin that isn't enabled for them); install silently double-enables a plugin already active at user scope; equip status/uninstall are blind to plugins enabled in settings files but absent from the manifest (supabase, impeccable) and report a clean bill (evidence items 15, 16, 19, 29, 34, 36, 39, 40, 41).
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED, three threads sharing one cause (equip only ever looks at its own manifest plus the single settings file it targets): (1) --scope freely lands wiring in settings.local.json (_settings_path_for_scope, discover.py:295-300) and the committed manifest records that gitignored path (:377-385) with no scope disclosure or warning — the SKILL.md documents project scope as the default but the CLI never explains the travel consequences of local. (2) enable_plugin checks only the target file; nothing reads ~/.claude/settings.json or the sibling settings file, so cross-scope duplicates are invisible. (3) status() and uninstall() iterate manifest.items only (status.py:152, :307) — enabledPlugins entries with no manifest record (native /plugin installs, hand-edits, dropped records) are never reported or disableable; missing_playbook (:157-158) likewise derives only from manifest items.
defects.proposed_fix: (1) On --scope local|user, print an explicit consequence line ('local scope: this wiring is per-machine and will NOT travel with the repo'); record the scope on the manifest item (new optional `scope: str|None` field on EquipmentItem, tolerated by the v3 loader) so status can label local-scope items. (2) Before enabling, scan the other scopes' settings files (project, local, ~/.claude/settings.json) for the same enabledPlugins key and warn 'already enabled at <scope> — installing again at <scope> duplicates it'. (3) Extend status(): scan .claude/settings.json + settings.local.json enabledPlugins and emit an `untracked` row for any plugin with no manifest item (state info, not error), so out-of-band installs become visible; offer `equip install --adopt <plugin>@<marketplace>` or document re-running install to adopt them (design decision: adopt verb vs documented re-install). (4) uninstall: also report (not silently skip) enabled-but-untracked plugins it cannot disable.
defects.tests_to_add: test_equip_discover_cli.py: local-scope install prints the does-not-travel warning and records scope='local'; duplicate-enable at another scope warns (monkeypatch Path.home for user scope). tests/context/domains/equip/test_equip_lifecycle_plugins.py: status over a fixture where settings.local.json enables a plugin absent from the manifest reports an 'untracked' row; uninstall reports it as not-disableable.

defects.symptom: No sanctioned CLI verb to repair or enrich a plugin's manifest entry (grounded_in, capabilities, subagent_type) — Claude performed raw Edit-tool surgery on the managed, hash-baselined equipment.json, twice; `equip patch` refuses plugin items (evidence items 26, 28; also the hand-edits in 6 and 24).
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. apply_patch gates on is_lifecycle_managed (evolve.py:41) — source==GENERATED with an origin_hash — so a MARKETPLACE item can never be patched; the EquipVerb alphabet (enums.py:25-41) and the dispatch surface (dispatch.py docstring verb list) contain no verb that mutates a plugin entry's metadata. The only post-install metadata channel is --usage-doc at install time; everything else requires hand-editing the managed JSON.
defects.proposed_fix: Add an `equip wire <plugin>@<marketplace> [--usage-doc PATH] [--capabilities a,b] [--subagent-type T]` verb: new EquipVerb.WIRE member, handler in cli/equip/discover.py (or a new wire.py beside it) that read_manifest→dataclasses.replace the matching MARKETPLACE/PLUGIN item→write_manifest. Reuse _validate_usage_doc so a grounded_in path must exist (kills dangling references at the source); validate capabilities against the Capability enum. Additionally have status() flag a manifest item whose grounded_in cites a nonexistent file ('dangling grounding') so hand-broken records surface. Design decision: verb name ('wire' vs 'ground' vs extending 'patch') — 'wire' matches the skill's vocabulary.
defects.tests_to_add: test_equip_discover_cli.py (or new test_equip_wire_cli.py): wire updates capabilities/grounded_in/subagent_type on an existing marketplace item and exits 1 typed-error on unknown item; --usage-doc pointing at a missing file exits 1; status flags an item whose grounded_in references a nonexistent path.

not_confirmed: Item 0 (plan ignored installed Supabase MCP; no '— via' annotation): already fixed at HEAD — commit 5c6be93 (2026-06-12) ships plan-time plugin annotation with installed-plugin inventory and auto equip-discover for gaps; the session ran a pre-0.24.0 skill vintage.

Item 5 (install leaves no .context grounding; user complained three times): substantially fixed at HEAD — commits e7ae87c + 56718cf (2026-06-10) make a usage playbook mandatory on install (--usage-doc/--skip-usage-doc, cli/equip/discover.py:163-205) and equip status flags marketplace items without one as 'incomplete (no usage playbook)' (status.py:157-158, verbs.py:68-69). Remaining metadata gaps are covered by the manifest and wire-verb defects above.

Items 25/26 (dangling grounded_in playbook that never existed; empty grounded_in despite an existing playbook): the production path is fixed at HEAD — _validate_usage_doc refuses a nonexistent file (cli/equip/discover.py:193-194), and empty grounded_in now surfaces as 'incomplete' in status. The dangling record in evidence came from Claude's hand-edited manifest after the install failure (see P0 resolution defect); a status-side dangling-grounding check is folded into the wire-verb defect.

Item 4 (SSH 'Permission denied (publickey)' cloning the plugin): the SSH-first clone URL is Claude Code's own plugin fetcher, not dummyindex code — no dummyindex defect; mitigation (HTTPS ls-remote preflight + warning) folded into the post-install-verification defect.

Items 8, 12, 13, 14, 17, 20 (build waves cannot execute plugin-routed/interactive items; '— via canvas-to-code:start' hand-simulated; 'Unknown skill' dispatch failures; wrong pages designed): build/plan orchestration territory — C7-build-routing cluster; only the manifest-side enabler (kind/capabilities/version/subagent_type) is handled here.
Item 11 (council enrichment expects an unwired Context7 MCP): council/enrichment cluster, not the plugin manager.
Item 7 (mid-session settings.json mutation conflated during verification): session-behavior noise; no dummyindex code defect identified.
Items 9, 10 (impeccable setup script MODULE_NOT_FOUND; NO_PRODUCT_MD init flow bypassed): defects/usage of the third-party impeccable plugin and agent behavior, not dummyindex code.
Item 37 (disabledMcpjsonServers lists 'framer' with no such .mcp.json server): stale config in the target repo; no dummyindex code path writes disabledMcpjsonServers.

Item 38 (eight vendored plugins under .claude/plugins/ not wired; README oversells): not produced by dummyindex — the vendor install path is explicitly 'a later slice' (cli/equip/discover.py:7); target-repo documentation issue.

Items 42-46 (Supabase CLI GATE not installed; apply_migration blast radius not communicated at build time; rejected migration had already executed; missing env wiring in proposal grounding; 92KB get_advisors overflow): plan/build execution-time issues — C7/C8 territory; the plugin manager's blast_radius.py only covers install-time disclosure, which functioned as designed.
quick_wins: cli/equip/discover.py:370 — drop `ref=chosen.plugin.version` from the add_marketplace call (the P0 wrong-ref fix is one line; tests a few more).
cli/equip/discover.py:405 + :397 area — set `version=chosen.plugin.version` on the recorded EquipmentItem so `equip status` stops rendering '(v-)' for plugins with a known catalog version.
cli/equip/discover.py:274 and :288 — suppress the 'add --repo <owner>/<name>' tip/footer when --repo was actually passed (thread a bool into _print_plan).
cli/equip/discover.py:282-285 — print the candidate's source repo (c.repo) on each discover row so a pinned/forked marketplace is identifiable.
context/domains/equip/constants.py:75 — move the 'audit' token out of the SECURITY row of _PLUGIN_CAPABILITY_TOKENS (it already exists in REVIEW), so design-audit plugins stop tagging 'security'.
context/domains/equip/plugins/discover.py:44-46 — filter stopwords/short tokens ('to','a','the','and','of','in','for','on','with') out of query tokens before counting hits.
cli/equip/discover.py:386 — append the activation guidance line: 'Claude Code loads plugins at session start — restart or run /reload-plugins, then verify with /plugin.'
